channel.title: Akin x Wumi
channel.description: Recent content on Akin x Wumi

item.title: Akin x Wumi
item.description: How far? 🤝 My name is Akin Wumi. I am a technologist and pan-Africanist. When I am not building Tools to solve problems, I am writings about my realities (mostly as an African).

item.title: Dear Akin
item.description: I have been thinking about you and having considered your antecedents and also the actions you have taken recently despite our numerous admonitions, I have decided to write this to you as a last recourse with the hope that it serves you well in the course of your life that is just beginning. Below, I highlighted 5 basic life principles some of which I have had to learn the hard way, and even though they are by no means exhaustive, I firmly believe that if you adopt them, you will stand in good stead regardless of the circumstances at any point in your life onwards.

item.title: My Thoughts On Balaji Srinivasan How To Start A New Country
item.description: I’ve been engrossed by the concept of a network state from an article I recently read by Balaji S. Srinivasan on how to start a new country. As a Nigerian who like many others is barely surviving within a failing state and whose nationality is a byproduct of colonialism, the possibility that I and countless others can escape the Nigerian contraption in a pragmatic way and be part of a thriving, supranational state that transcends physical boundaries is irresistible and here are my thoughts:

item.title: The meek shall not inherit Nigeria
item.description: In a world where politics and power struggles are constantly at play, one man dedicated his life to uncovering the secrets of cooperation and competition. Robert Axelrod was a brilliant political scientist whose groundbreaking research on the dynamics of human behavior has proven invaluable time and time again. In his experiments, Axelrod created a virtual game that pitted individuals against each other in a battle of self-interest versus cooperation. The rules were simple, yet the implications were profound: to win, players had to find the optimal balance between cooperation and competition.

item.title: Tools
item.description: Abacus Yababot Streakbot Forfinders Nairalife Trivia9ja Hifofin Token.ng Bookmakr Sendcash Pay PortalNG

item.title: Writings
item.description: The meek shall not inherit Nigeria XXVIII XXVII My Thoughts On Balaji Srinivasan How To Start A New Country Dear Akin XXVI

item.title: XXVI
item.description: Sometimes on your birthday, you can have the most profound awareness. For example on 7/11/2012 which was in my second year at the university, I came to the conclusion that schooling in Unilorin was not just terrible but also irredeemable and the prudent thing is to quit the system regardless of the sunk cost. To get in and out of the university campus, for instance, you’d have to spend several hours on queue jostling for scarce taxis with most of your fellow students.

item.title: XXVII
item.description: Reflecting on the period between Nov 8, 2020 — November 8, 2021 It was a very tough year chai 😭. Apart from the days of running Token Nigeria, the middle of this period was probably the nadir of my life so far. Sometime between September 2020 and December 2020, I and wisdom worked and iterated on the initial version of Bookmakr - an odds comparison tool that morphed into a booking code aggregation and conversion feed - then we started 2021 by making it publicly available but within 4 months, we had to shut it down even though it was one of the most difficult things we had ever built.

item.title: XXVIII
item.description: Reflecting on the period between January 1, 2022 — January 31, 2023 Writing this has been a struggle for the last two months or so, and it wasn’t until yesterday morning when I reminded myself of why I do this, that I found some impetus. This is not a vainglorious project. On the contrary, it is mainly an attempt to preserve my mental state over a yearly transition so that when I look back, I will have a film of data to go through.
